title: gastrolobium effusum
intro: Gastrolobium effusum, also known as Poison Peak or Oenothera, is a species of flowering plant in the family Fabaceae. Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antispasmodic, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gastrolobium effusum include relieving pain, reducing inflammation, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Gastrolobium effusum can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or taken internally under the guidance of a healthcare professional.
